name: Discord MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server integrating with Discord to provide AI-powered features for Discord servers. Enables AI agents to read messages, send responses, manage channels, and interact with Discord server content — supporting Discord community management and moderation AI workflows.
use_cases: Reading Discord channel messages and threads from community monitoring agents
Sending automated responses and notifications to Discord channels from AI assistants
Managing Discord server content and channel interactions from moderation agents
Building AI-powered Discord bots that respond to community queries
not_for: Automated spam or unsolicited messaging (prohibited by Discord)
best_when: A community manager or developer wants AI agents to help monitor, moderate, or interact with a Discord server — automating routine community management tasks.
avoid_when: Your team uses Slack or Teams. Also: Discord bot terms of service apply — ensure your use case complies with Discord's developer policy.

auth.notes: Discord Bot Token required. Bot must be added to server with appropriate permissions. Use minimal bot permissions for agent use cases.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Discord API is free for bots; rate limits apply
pricing.notes: Discord API is free. Bot token required. Community MCP implementation.

performance.rate_limits: Discord API rate limits apply: varies by endpoint (typically 50 requests/second globally)

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Discord messages may contain personal conversations — restrict to specific channels
Discord ToS compliance required — ensure bot usage follows developer policy
Discord bot permissions must be carefully scoped — use minimal permissions
Rate limiting is per-route in Discord API — implement proper backoff
Message sending by agents could be perceived as spam — use sparingly
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Community message access. Bot token must be secured. Discord ToS compliance required. Restrict bot permissions to minimum needed.

security_score_components.security_notes: Community message privacy. Bot token security. Minimal permissions principle. Discord ToS compliance required.
